headline: ConocoPhillips Weighs Syria Offshore MoU Against Valuation And Risk Profile
summary: ConocoPhillips (NYSE:COP), together with TotalEnergies and QatarEnergy, has signed a Memorandum of Understanding with the Syrian Petroleum Company. The agreement covers potential offshore exploration in Syrian waters in the Mediterranean. This move introduces ConocoPhillips to a new exploration area that carries both resource and geopolitical considerations. For investors tracking NYSE:COP, this offshore Syria agreement comes as the stock trades around $120.46 per share. The stock is up...

headline: Alaska’s Oil Revival Sparks a New Energy Rush Into the Arctic
summary: (Bloomberg) -- When John Kurz left Alaska’s North Slope in 2009, he was staring at a grim future for what had once been the country’s premiere oil field.
